title: GitHub Actions: Ubuntu: Podman build failed: Connection reset by peer
body:
There is a critical but in your latest version on 
Ubuntu version: 22.04
Podman version: 3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1
The command that failed:
`podman build -t quay.io/ebattat/benchmark-runner:latest .`
The error:
`error running container: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c dnf update -y --nobest]: sd-bus call: Connection reset by peer #1994`

Connection reset by peer
Got the following error, while running container
The full GitHub actions [link](https://github.com/redhat-performance/benchmark-runner/runs/7306853603?check_suite_focus=true)

The same workflow run successful several days ago, [link](https://github.com/redhat-performance/benchmark-runner/runs/7229627245?check_suite_focus=true)

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @ebattat.

> There is a critical but in your latest version on Ubuntu version: 22.04 Podman version: 3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1 The command that failed: `podman build -t quay.io/ebattat/benchmark-runner:latest .` The error: `error running container: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c dnf update -y --nobest]: sd-bus call: Connection reset by peer #1994`

I cannot reproduce on the latest Ubuntu 22.04, so I assume there is something wrong in the GitHub action.  Did you try your build on a vanilla Ubuntu 22.04 (i.e., outside the action)?

> Connection reset by peer Got the following error, while running container The full GitHub actions [link](https://github.com/redhat-performance/benchmark-runner/runs/7306853603?check_suite_focus=true)

The link does not work.

Please make sure to fill out the entire issue template next time.  Especially the output of `podman info --debug` is crucial for understanding many issues.

`sd-bus call` sounds like an issue connecting to systemd, please make sure you use a proper login session when running rootless.

This does not look related to podman, more like a issue with your environment. We also cannot really support older version please try with the latest version of podman and crun or try runc instead of crun since crun is returning the error here.

Yes,
I have tried it on my local and also reproduced this issue:
The steps:
podman run --rm -it --name ubuntu_podman ubuntu:latest
Run inside the container:
$ apt-get -y update
$ apt-get -y install podman
$ apt-get -y install git vim
$  vi Dockerfile
```
FROM quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
RUN dnf update -y
```
root@9105c2fbdfe0:/# podman build -t test . 
STEP 1/2: FROM quay.io/centos/centos:stream8
STEP 2/2: RUN dnf update -y
error running container: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c dnf update -y]: mount `/dev` to '/dev': Permission denied
: exit status 1
Error: error building at STEP "RUN dnf update -y": error while running runtime: exit status 1


Please let me know that you are able to reproduce it.
Running inside a container requires more setup. see https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container

Looks that latest update of podman inside ubuntu container in GitHub Actions cause it.
Can I ugrade podman version or install manually 3.4.4 instead of 3.4.2 ?

Steps to reproduce

```
name: Podman test
on:
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  bundle:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Create container
        run: |
          cat <<EOF | sudo podman build -t test-container -f - .
          FROM fedora:36
          RUN echo test
          EOF
Output:

STEP 1/2: FROM fedora:36
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:36...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:75f075168a24344c81b41a4877ffc6005723793ced6424f20b5f3ee9d6a66b57
Copying blob sha256:75f075168a24344c81b41a4877ffc6005723793ced6424f20b5f3ee9d6a66b57
Copying config sha256:3a66698e604003f7822a0c73e9da50e090fda9a99fe1f2e1e2e7fe796cc803d5
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2/2: RUN echo test
error running container: error from /usr/bin/crun creating container for [/bin/sh -c echo test]: sd-bus call: Connection reset by peer
: exit status 1
```
I'm seeing the same error with Lorax's tests - https://github.com/weldr/lorax/runs/7327737690?check_suite_focus=true

Till GitHub actions team will solve this issue there are 2 workarounds only for build: (for more details [see](https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1994))
1.**Workaround for GitHub actions till fix: **
sudo -E XDG_RUNTIME_DIR= podman build -t test-container -f - .
2. Use docker instead of podman

I think that Podman team should be synchronized with GitHub actions team regarding issue like that.
In my use case with lorax I fixed this by dropping sudo from the workflow - https://github.com/weldr/lorax/commit/05a4182d1bd435dd0d157e71221b28eb4a6bdd58

@mheon This problem looks related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14603.
The current workaround is to use `sudo -E XDG_RUNTIME_DIR= podman ...`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I believe this is now fixed, or not something Podman needs to/can fix.

Closing, reopen if I am mistaken.

Think I'm seeing this on MS Azure agents - i think the XDG_RUNTIME_DIR is needed if connecting to an external repository? @rhatdan (the auth details after login are stored in `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json`)
